a:T22a3,<div class="specification">
<p>A school consists of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>740</mn></math> students divided into <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn></math> grade levels. The numbers of students in each grade are shown in the table below.</p>
<p><img src="data:image/png;base64," style="display: block; margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto;"/></p>
<p>The Principal of the school wishes to select a sample of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>25</mn></math> students. She wishes to ensure that, as closely as possible, the proportion of the students from each grade in the sample is the same as the proportions in the school.</p>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Calculate the number of grade <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>12</mn></math> students who should be in the sample.</p>
<div class="marks">[3]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">a.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>The Principal selects the students for the sample by asking those who took part in a previous survey if they would like to take part in another. She takes the first of those who reply positively, up to the maximum needed for the sample.</p>
<p>State which two of the sampling methods listed below best describe the method used.</p>
<p>Stratified          Quota          Convenience          Systematic          Simple random</p>
<div class="marks">[2]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">b.</div>
</div>b:T43f,<div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p style="color:#999;font-size:90%;font-style:italic;">* This sample question was produced by experienced DP mathematics senior examiners to aid teachers in preparing for external assessment in the new MAA course. There may be minor differences in formatting compared to formal exam papers.</p>
<p><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mn>181</mn><mn>740</mn></mfrac><mo>×</mo><mn>25</mn><mo>=</mo><mn>6</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>11486</mn><mo>…</mo></math>         <strong>M1(A1)</strong></p>
<p><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6</mn></math> (students)        <strong>A1</strong></p>
<p> </p>
<p><strong>[3 marks]</strong></p>
<div class="question_part_label">a.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>Quota and convenience       <strong>A1A1</strong></p>
<p> </p>
<p><strong>Note:</strong> Award <strong>A1A0</strong> for one correct and one incorrect answer.</p>
<p> </p>
<p><strong>[2 marks]</strong></p>
<div class="question_part_label">b.</div>
</div>2:["$","article",null,{"className":"q-page","children":[["$","p",null,{"style":{"margin":0},"children":["$","$L6",null,{"href":"/subject/maths/topic/4/","children":["← back to ","maths"," topic ","4"]}]}],["$","h1",null,{"children":"EXN.1.SL.TZ0.1"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-meta","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"tag","children":"pestle"}],["$","span",null,{"children":"maths"}],["$","span",null,{"children":"AI"}],["$","span",null,{"children":"SL"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["Paper ","1"]}],["$","span",null,{"children":"EXN"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["· ","sl-4-1-concepts-reliability-and-sampling-techniques"]}],["$","a",null,{"href":"https://pestle.pages.dev/app/","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener","children":"source ↗"}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-content","dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$a"}}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Markscheme / solution"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$b"}}]]}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Examiners’ report"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n[N/A]\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">a.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n[N/A]\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">b.</div>\n</div>"}}]]}]]}]
